data.agent_description: An AI agent specializing in cybersecurity analysis that processes large volumes of security telemetry, logs, alerts, vulnerability data, threat intelligence, and asset context to detect threats, identify vulnerabilities, prioritize risks, and generate actionable insights for security and business stakeholders.
data.summary:
Agent Risk Assessment Summary
==============================
Agent Name:       Cybersecurity Analysis Agent
Agent Version:    None
Agent Owner Name: Admin
 
The agent was assessed based on the EU AI Act and the AI Vulnerability Scoring System (AIVSS).
The agent is designated as 'Other' under the EU AI Act, a classification reinforced by
an AIVSS score of None/10, placing it in the 'None Risk' category.
A detailed breakdown of the assessment is provided in the following sections.
 
==============================
Regulatory Risk Summary
==============================
This is the classification of the agent based on the EU AI Act:
 
Risk Classification:                    Other
Personally Identifiable Information:    No
Protected Health Information:           No
Payment Card Industry:                  No
 
EU AI Act Article 5 (Prohibited AI Practices) Evaluation:
  Subliminal and Manipulative Techniques:    No
  Exploitation of Vulnerabilities:           No
  Social Scoring Systems:                    No
  Risk Assessment for Criminal Offences:     No
  Facial Recognition Database Creation:      No
  Emotion Inference in Workplace/Education:  No
  Biometric Categorisation:                  No
  Real-Time Remote Biometric Identification: No
 
EU AI Act Article 6 (High-Risk AI Systems) Evaluation:
  Biometrics:                                                          No
  Critical Infrastructure:                                             No
  Education and Vocational Training:                                   No
  Employment, Workers' Management and Access to Self-Employment:       No
  Access to Essential Private/Public Services and Benefits:            No
  Law Enforcement:                                                     No
  Migration, Asylum and Border Control Management:                     No
  Administration of Justice and Democratic Processes:                  No
  Safety Component of a Product:                                       No
  Medical Devices:                                                     No
  In Vitro Diagnostic Medical Devices:                                 No
  Other High Risk Items:                                               No
 
Risk Classification Rationale:
The Cybersecurity Analysis Agent processes large volumes of security telemetry, logs, and threat intelligence to detect threats, identify vulnerabilities, and prioritize risks. It does not engage in any prohibited AI practices listed under Article 5 of the EU AI Act such as subliminal techniques, exploitation of vulnerabilities, social scoring, biometric categorisation, or real-time biometric identification. Additionally, it is not classified as a high-risk AI system under Article 6, as it does not pertain to biometrics, critical infrastructure management, employment decisions, law enforcement, migration, administration of justice, medical devices, or other enumerated high-risk categories. The agent does not handle protected health information or payment card data, and there is no explicit mention or indication that personally identifiable information (PII) is processed. Therefore, the risk classification is 'Other' according to the EU AI Act criteria.
 
==============================
OWASP AIVSS Summary
==============================
OWASP Agentic AI Risk Score (AARS) Capability Summary:
AIVSS Score for Cybersecurity Analysis Agent: None
AARS Score: 0.00
 
+--------------------------+-------+----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| AARS Capability          | Score | Rationale                                                                                                                                                                                    |
+--------------------------+-------+----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Autonomy of Action       | 0.00  | Explicit in metadata: 'Do not take disruptive response actions... without explicit human authorization'. Autonomy is gated with human in the loop.                                           |
| Contextual Awareness     | 0.00  | No explicit information found for Contextual Awareness within the agent details; no indication of real-time internet or external data source connectivity.                                   |
| Dynamic Identity         | 0.00  | No explicit information found for Dynamic Identity within the agent details; no mention of role changes or permissions escalation by the agent.                                              |
| Dynamic Tool Use         | 0.00  | No explicit information found for Tool Use within the agent details; restricted to internal processing without mention of external dynamic tool use or write capabilities.                   |
| Goal-Driven Planning     | 0.00  | No explicit information found for Goal-Driven Planning within the agent details; no evidence of multi-step or recursive planning capabilities.                                               |
| Memory Use               | 0.00  | No explicit information found for Memory Use within the agent details; no mention of persistent or writable memory influencing future behaviour.                                             |
| Multi-Agent Interactions | 0.00  | No explicit information found for Multi-Agent Interactions within the agent details; no mention of agent communication or negotiation with other agents.                                     |
| Non-Determinism          | 0.00  | No explicit information found for Non-Determinism within the agent details; outputs seem structured and deterministic based on data inputs and protocols.                                    |
| Self-Modification        | 0.00  | No explicit information found for Self-Modification within the agent details; no indication of ability to alter own code or models.                                                          |
| Opacity & Reflexivity    | 0.00  | Explicit presence of auditability and logging: 'Preserve auditability by recording data sources, correlation logic, assumptions, timestamps, and analyst decisions' indicating transparency. |
+--------------------------+-------+----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
 
==============================
OWASP Agent Risk Scenario Summary
==============================
Agent Risk Scenario Summary
The following OWASP Scenario-Level Risks were assessed:
 
+------+--------------------------------------------------+-------------+-------------------+------------+
| S.No | Risk Scenario                                    | AIVSS Score | Threat Multiplier | AARS Score |
+------+--------------------------------------------------+-------------+-------------------+------------+
| 1    | Agentic AI Tool Misuse                           | 1.05        | 1.00              | 1.05       |
| 2    | Agent Access Control Violation                   | 1.05        | 1.00              | 1.05       |
| 3    | Agent Cascading Failures                         | 1.05        | 1.00              | 1.05       |
| 4    | Agent Orchestration and Multi-Agent Exploitation | 1.05        | 1.00              | 1.05       |
| 5    | Agent Identity Impersonation                     | 1.05        | 1.00              | 1.05       |
| 6    | Insecure Agent Critical Systems Interaction      | 1.05        | 1.00              | 1.05       |
| 7    | Agent Memory and Context Manipulation            | 1.05        | 1.00              | 1.05       |
| 8    | Agent Supply Chain and Dependency Attacks        | 1.05        | 1.00              | 1.05       |
| 9    | Agent Untraceability                             | 1.05        | 1.00              | 1.05       |
| 10   | Agent Goal and Instruction Manipulation          | 1.05        | 1.00              | 1.05       |
+------+--------------------------------------------------+-------------+-------------------+------------+

data.agent_description: End-to-end predictive maintenance agent for Oil & Gas upstream and downstream operations. Continuously ingests sensor telemetry (vibration, temperature, pressure, flow, motor current) from rotating and static equipment via OSIsoft PI historian, applies a multi-model ML ensemble (LSTM autoencoder for anomaly detection, XGBoost for fault classification, Weibull RUL estimator) to predict equipment failure windows with confidence scoring, and autonomously triggers CMMS work-order creation, ERP spare-parts reservation, and maintenance supervisor notifications for non-PSM assets. For all assets flagged SafetyCritical=true under OSHA PSM (29 CFR 1910.119), the agent strictly operates in advisory-only mode — generating detailed recommendations but taking no autonomous action without explicit qualified engineer approval. Every prediction, action, and outcome is written to an immutable audit log conforming to API RP 754 Tier 3 process safety performance indicator format, enabling full agentic lineage reconstruction. Post-maintenance outcome feedback is captured for continuous model retraining. The agent is segregated from all DCS setpoint control, safety instrumented systems (SIS), and process alarms — it operates exclusively as a monitoring and workflow-triggering layer.
data.summary:
Agent Risk Assessment Summary
==============================
Agent Name:       Equipment Health Agent
Agent Version:    0.3.0
Agent Owner Name: Admin
 
The agent was assessed based on the EU AI Act and the AI Vulnerability Scoring System (AIVSS).
The agent is designated as 'High Risk' under the EU AI Act, a classification reinforced by
an AIVSS score of None/10, placing it in the 'None Risk' category.
A detailed breakdown of the assessment is provided in the following sections.
 
==============================
Regulatory Risk Summary
==============================
This is the classification of the agent based on the EU AI Act:
 
Risk Classification:                    High Risk
Personally Identifiable Information:    No
Protected Health Information:           No
Payment Card Industry:                  No
 
EU AI Act Article 5 (Prohibited AI Practices) Evaluation:
  Subliminal and Manipulative Techniques:    No
  Exploitation of Vulnerabilities:           No
  Social Scoring Systems:                    No
  Risk Assessment for Criminal Offences:     No
  Facial Recognition Database Creation:      No
  Emotion Inference in Workplace/Education:  No
  Biometric Categorisation:                  No
  Real-Time Remote Biometric Identification: No
 
EU AI Act Article 6 (High-Risk AI Systems) Evaluation:
  Biometrics:                                                          No
  Critical Infrastructure:                                             Yes
  Education and Vocational Training:                                   No
  Employment, Workers' Management and Access to Self-Employment:       No
  Access to Essential Private/Public Services and Benefits:            No
  Law Enforcement:                                                     No
  Migration, Asylum and Border Control Management:                     No
  Administration of Justice and Democratic Processes:                  No
  Safety Component of a Product:                                       No
  Medical Devices:                                                     No
  In Vitro Diagnostic Medical Devices:                                 No
  Other High Risk Items:                                               No
 
Risk Classification Rationale:
The Equipment Health Agent operates in the Oil & Gas industry, which is classified under critical infrastructure management as per Article 6 of the EU AI Act. It functions as a predictive maintenance tool that continuously monitors and assesses operational equipment to prevent failures and reduce downtime. Although it does not interact with biometric data, personal data, or healthcare data, it plays a vital role in managing critical infrastructure, placing it under the High Risk category. There are no indicators that it involves any prohibited AI practices of Article 5, hence no Prohibited classification is warranted.
 
==============================
OWASP AIVSS Summary
==============================
OWASP Agentic AI Risk Score (AARS) Capability Summary:
AIVSS Score for Equipment Health Agent: None
AARS Score: 4.00
 
+--------------------------+-------+----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| AARS Capability          | Score | Rationale                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
+--------------------------+-------+----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Autonomy of Action       | 0.00  | The agent requires explicit human approval for work order creation on SafetyCritical assets and operates in advisory-only mode in that case. Other autonomous actions are constrained by hard-coded rules and APIs without arbitrary code execution.     |
| Contextual Awareness     | 0.50  | The agent adapts its behavior based on real-time sensor data, asset metadata including safety flags, and data quality, demonstrating curated external data dependence but no unrestricted internet access.                                               |
| Dynamic Identity         | 0.50  | The agent changes its operation mode and permissions (advisory-only vs autonomous) based on asset safety status and requires human approval for certain assets, indicating governed delegation of roles.                                                 |
| Dynamic Tool Use         | 0.50  | The agent dynamically uses multiple external APIs (OSIsoft PI historian, SAP_PM_API, ERP_Inventory_API, Notification_API, Audit_Log_API) with write capabilities for work orders and notifications, but is limited to predefined APIs and schemas.       |
| Goal-Driven Planning     | 1.00  | The agent executes a complex multi-step workflow including continuous data ingestion, multi-model ensemble processing, decision branching for advisory or autonomous actions, and workflow initiation, showing orchestrated multi-step planning.         |
| Memory Use               | 1.00  | The agent captures post-maintenance feedback and triggers continuous model retraining with persistent storage to a Model_Retraining_API and maintains an immutable audit log, evidencing persistent writable memory use.                                 |
| Multi-Agent Interactions | 0.50  | The agent coordinates with external maintenance supervisors, engineers, and relies on APIs to notify and receive approval for actions, evidencing orchestrated static communications but no autonomous dynamic discovery or negotiation with new agents. |
| Non-Determinism          | 0.00  | The agent's inputs and outputs operate under strict schemas with structured anomaly detection and classification models, and all outputs and actions are logged, indicating deterministic behavior.                                                      |
| Self-Modification        | 0.00  | No indication of self-modification or runtime changes to source code, models, or logic beyond persistent feedback storage is provided; updates are external and manual.                                                                                  |
| Opacity & Reflexivity    | 0.00  | The agent maintains a detailed immutable audit log including model versions, sensor data, classifications, and decisions conforming to API RP 754 Tier 3, evidencing transparent and deterministic internal reasoning logs.                              |
+--------------------------+-------+----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
 
==============================
OWASP Agent Risk Scenario Summary
==============================
Agent Risk Scenario Summary
The following OWASP Scenario-Level Risks were assessed:
 
+------+--------------------------------------------------+-------------+-------------------+------------+
| S.No | Risk Scenario                                    | AIVSS Score | Threat Multiplier | AARS Score |
+------+--------------------------------------------------+-------------+-------------------+------------+
| 1    | Agentic AI Tool Misuse                           | 3.05        | 1.00              | 3.05       |
| 2    | Agent Access Control Violation                   | 3.05        | 1.00              | 3.05       |
| 3    | Agent Cascading Failures                         | 3.05        | 1.00              | 3.05       |
| 4    | Agent Orchestration and Multi-Agent Exploitation | 3.05        | 1.00              | 3.05       |

| 6    | Insecure Agent Critical Systems Interaction      | 3.05        | 1.00              | 3.05       |
| 7    | Agent Memory and Context Manipulation            | 3.05        | 1.00              | 3.05       |
| 8    | Agent Supply Chain and Dependency Attacks        | 3.05        | 1.00              | 3.05       |
| 9    | Agent Untraceability                             | 3.05        | 1.00              | 3.05       |
| 10   | Agent Goal and Instruction Manipulation          | 3.05        | 1.00              | 3.05       |
+------+--------------------------------------------------+-------------+-------------------+------------+

data.agent_name: AURORA-ADVISE: Decision Support, Reporting & Alerting Agent
data.agent_description: AURORA-ADVISE is the human-facing delivery agent of the AURORA system. It receives Decision Packages from AURORA-PREDICT and translates them into actionable engineer notifications, tiered alerts, and automated operational reports. It manages the full communication and documentation workflow — ensuring drilling engineers, operations managers, and rig crews receive timely, contextual, and prioritized guidance. It also generates automated Daily Drilling Reports (DDRs) and performance dashboards directly from OSDU data, eliminating manual report authoring.
data.summary:
Agent Risk Assessment Summary
==============================
Agent Name:       AURORA-ADVISE: Decision Support, Reporting & Alerting Agent
Agent Version:    0.3.0
Agent Owner Name: Admin
 
The agent was assessed based on the EU AI Act and the AI Vulnerability Scoring System (AIVSS).
The agent is designated as 'Other' under the EU AI Act, a classification reinforced by
an AIVSS score of 2.05/10, placing it in the 'Low Risk' category.
A detailed breakdown of the assessment is provided in the following sections.
 
==============================
Regulatory Risk Summary
==============================
This is the classification of the agent based on the EU AI Act:
 
Risk Classification:                    Other
Personally Identifiable Information:    No
Protected Health Information:           No
Payment Card Industry:                  No
 
EU AI Act Article 5 (Prohibited AI Practices) Evaluation:
  Subliminal and Manipulative Techniques:    No

  Risk Assessment for Criminal Offences:     No

  Real-Time Remote Biometric Identification: No
 
EU AI Act Article 6 (High-Risk AI Systems) Evaluation:

  Critical Infrastructure:                                             No

  Employment, Workers' Management and Access to Self-Employment:       No
  Access to Essential Private/Public Services and Benefits:            No
  Law Enforcement:                                                     No
  Migration, Asylum and Border Control Management:                     No
  Administration of Justice and Democratic Processes:                  No
  Safety Component of a Product:                                       No
  Medical Devices:                                                     No
  In Vitro Diagnostic Medical Devices:                                 No
  Other High Risk Items:                                               No
 
Risk Classification Rationale:
The AURORA-ADVISE agent functions as a decision support, reporting, and alerting tool specifically for drilling engineers and operations management, using drilling and operational data. It does not involve any prohibited AI practices outlined in Article 5, such as subliminal techniques, exploitation of vulnerabilities, social scoring, biometric categorization, or real-time remote biometric identification. It also does not fall under any of the high-risk AI system categories in Article 6, including biometrics, critical infrastructure safety components, education, employment, essential services, law enforcement, migration, administration of justice, medical devices, or other high-risk categories. The system primarily processes operational and engineering performance data and does not explicitly process personally identifiable information, protected health information, or payment card data. Therefore, following the Article 5 and 6 criteria and data sensitivity rules, it is classified as 'Other' risk level.
 
==============================
OWASP AIVSS Summary
==============================
OWASP Agentic AI Risk Score (AARS) Capability Summary:
AIVSS Score for AURORA-ADVISE: Decision Support, Reporting & Alerting Agent: 2.05
AARS Score: 2.00
 
+--------------------------+-------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| AARS Capability          | Score | Rationale                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
+--------------------------+-------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Autonomy of Action       | 0.00  | 
The metadata explicitly states that all parameter changes require explicit engineer confirmation and the agent never modifies drilling system setpoints or parameters autonomously, indicating fully gated autonomy.
                                           |
| Contextual Awareness     | 0.00  | 
No explicit information found for Contextual Awareness within the agent details.
                                                                                                                                                                               |
| Dynamic Identity         | 0.00  | 
No explicit information found for Dynamic Identity within the agent details.
                                                                                                                                                                                   |
| Dynamic Tool Use         | 0.50  | 
The agent uses predefined tools like dashboards, SMS/email notifications and integrates with rig communications, and writes reports to OSDU DrillReport WPC. This represents sandboxed and bounded tool use with write access limited to structured reporting.
 |
| Goal-Driven Planning     | 0.50  | 
The agent processes multi-step workflows (receive decision packages, classify alerts, route, track responses, escalate, generate reports), indicating internal multi-step goal decomposition.
                                                                  |
| Memory Use               | 0.50  | 
The agent generates automated reports and tracks engineer acknowledgments and KPIs, and writes structured report outputs back to a persistent system (OSDU DrillReport WPC), indicating persistent writable memory use.
                                        |
| Multi-Agent Interactions | 0.00  | 
No explicit information found for Multi Agent Interactions within the agent details.
                                                                                                                                                                           |
| Non-Determinism          | 0.00  | 
The agent's input and output appear structured and deterministic, involving classification and routing of alerts, and generating structured reports, indicating deterministic behaviour.
                                                                       |
| Self-Modification        | 0.00  | 
No explicit information found for Self Modification within the agent details.
                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| Opacity & Reflexivity    | 0.50  | 
The agent ensures auditability and data lineage by writing structured report outputs back to OSDU DrillReport WPC, indicating summarised, high-level reasoning exposure rather than full transparency or black-box opacity.
                                    |
+--------------------------+-------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+

OWASP Agent Risk Scenario Summary

data.agent_description: Your main role is to predict equipment availability and restoration timelines during infrastructure failures caused by severe weather events.
data.summary:
Agent Risk Assessment Summary
==============================
Agent Name:       Grid Resilience Supply Chain Agent
Agent Version:    0.3.0
Agent Owner Name: Admin
 
The agent was assessed based on the EU AI Act and the AI Vulnerability Scoring System (AIVSS).
The agent is designated as 'High Risk' under the EU AI Act, a classification reinforced by
an AIVSS score of 1.55/10, placing it in the 'Low Risk' category.
A detailed breakdown of the assessment is provided in the following sections.
 
==============================
Regulatory Risk Summary
==============================
This is the classification of the agent based on the EU AI Act:
 
Risk Classification:                    High Risk
Personally Identifiable Information:    No
Protected Health Information:           No
Payment Card Industry:                  No
 
EU AI Act Article 5 (Prohibited AI Practices) Evaluation:
  Subliminal and Manipulative Techniques:    No

  In Vitro Diagnostic Medical Devices:                                 No

Risk Classification Rationale:
The Grid Resilience Supply Chain Agent is designed to predict equipment availability and restoration timelines during infrastructure failures caused by severe weather events, directly supporting the management and operation of critical electricity infrastructure. According to Article 6 of the EU AI Act, AI systems intended for use in critical infrastructure management and operation are classified as high-risk. The system does not involve any prohibited AI practices under Article 5, nor does it process personally identifiable information, protected health information, or payment card information. Therefore, the appropriate classification is 'High Risk' based on its critical infrastructure application.
 
==============================
OWASP AIVSS Summary
==============================

AIVSS Score for Grid Resilience Supply Chain Agent: 1.55
AARS Score: 1.00

+--------------------------+-------+-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Autonomy of Action       | 0.00  | 
The metadata and memories explicitly state that the agent "does not automatically initiate procurement or logistics actions without human approval" and requires "tiered automated response authority bounded by the three-tier protocol," indicating fully gated autonomy.
 |

No explicit information found for Contextual Awareness within the agent details.
                                                                                                                                                                                            |

No explicit information found for Dynamic Identity within the agent details.

data.agent_description: A unified AI agent that manages the full lifecycle of syndicated loan document processing. It ingests credit agreements (PDF/DOCX), extracts loan reference data including SOFR and floating rate instruments, validates against the Loan Administration System, reconciles discrepancies, routes exceptions to operations teams, and emits a data quality report — all in a single orchestrated workflow with built-in immutable audit logging.

data.agent_description: Safety-related, 24/7 autonomous AI agent providing continuous multi-modal monitoring of Oil & Gas pipelines, process facilities, tank farms, and offshore platforms for the earliest possible detection of hydrocarbon leaks, toxic gas releases, flammable gas releases, pressure anomalies, and environmental emissions exceedances. The agent fuses data from five independent sensor modalities — fiber-optic DTS temperature profiling, acoustic emission arrays, fixed-point gas detectors, SCADA pressure/flow balance, and CEMS — using a Bayesian multi-sensor fusion architecture that requires dual-source corroboration before triggering any automated action, thereby minimising false positives while maintaining detection sensitivity. Response actions follow a formally defined three-tier protocol: Tier 1 (alert operator, always immediate, all events), Tier 2 (autonomous isolation valve closure on non-SIS, non-PSM assets with dual-sensor confirmed event at confidence >85%, pre-authorised by operations engineering review), Tier 3 (mandatory human Incident Commander authorisation for all PSM-covered assets, SIS-adjacent events, or emergency shutdowns — agent never acts autonomously on Tier 3). The agent additionally aggregates CEMS data in real time against EPA permit limits, generates proactive advisories when sources approach 90% of permit limits, and produces pre-formatted regulatory compliance reports for EPA 40 CFR, GRI 305, SASB EM-EP, and OGMP 2.0 methane framework. Every event — from initial sensor anomaly through detection, corroboration, action, and resolution — generates an immutable forensic evidence package conforming to PHMSA 49 CFR Part 195.446 record-keeping requirements. The agent is the primary automated safety guardian of the enterprise's physical asset network and is therefore classified as a safety-related AI system subject to enhanced governance, mandatory human escalation for high-consequence events, and strict OT/ICS cybersecurity controls per IEC 62443.

Risk Classification Rationale:
The Safety Environmental Monitor Agent does not fall under any prohibited practices outlined in Article 5 of the EU AI Act, such as subliminal techniques or biometric categorisation. However, it is designed as a safety-related AI system for continuous monitoring and tiered autonomous response within critical infrastructure — specifically oil and gas pipelines and related assets — which aligns with the definitions of high-risk AI systems under Article 6. The agent acts as a safety component of the critical infrastructure product, managing and safeguarding physical assets, hence the fields for Critical Infrastructure and Safety Component of a Product in Article 6 are marked 'Yes'. There is no indication that it processes personally identifiable information (PII), protected health information (PHI), or payment card information (PCI). Therefore, per the classification cascade of the EU AI Act, this system is classified as 'High Risk' due to its deployment in critical infrastructure and safety-critical applications.

All API calls and write operations are hard-coded with no capability to generate new queries or arbitrary URLs beyond schema.                              |

Interactions are with external APIs and human operators only; no multi-agent negotiation or dynamic discovery described.                                                                                                                     |

data.agent_description: Real-time advisory AI agent for well drilling operations across upstream Oil & Gas. Continuously ingests downhole and surface drilling telemetry at up to 10 Hz via WITSML/WITS protocol — including weight-on-bit (WOB), rate of penetration (ROP), rotary torque, standpipe pressure, equivalent circulating density (ECD), annular pressure, mud flow-in/flow-out differential, pit volume gains, hook load, and motor current — and applies physics-informed LSTM neural networks to detect early well-control events (kicks, losses, stuck pipe, washouts, bit balling) up to 30 minutes before traditional surface detection methods. The agent integrates with a real-time digital twin well simulator (Halliburton WellPlan or Schlumberger OLGA) to validate parameter recommendations through forward scenario modelling before presenting to the driller. Formation evaluation API integration provides proactive mud-weight window updates as the drill bit approaches formation tops. All recommendations are advisory-only — the agent has zero write access to BOP control systems, top-drive PLCs, mud pump controllers, or any SIS/ESD. Driller and toolpusher responses (accept/reject/modify) are logged for agentic lineage and model retraining. For HIGH severity well-control events (pit gain > 15 bbl or flow-out delta > 30 gpm), the agent sounds the rig alarm and notifies all personnel without waiting for driller acknowledgement — this is the single pre-authorised autonomous action. Every event and recommendation is written to an immutable drilling audit log for BSEE and OSHA forensic investigation capability.

EU AI Act Article 5 (Prohibited AI Practices) Evaluation:
  Subliminal and Manipulative Techniques:    No

  Medical Devices:                                                     No
  In Vitro Diagnostic Medical Devices:                                 No
  Other High Risk Items:                                               No
 
Risk Classification Rationale:
The Drilling Optimisation Agent is classified as High Risk because it operates within critical infrastructure, specifically upstream Oil & Gas drilling operations that are essential to energy supply and infrastructure safety. According to Article 6 of the EU AI Act, AI systems used as safety components in the management and operation of critical digital infrastructure or supply systems such as water, gas, heating, or electricity are considered high-risk. While this agent is advisory-only and does not actuate control systems directly, it provides safety-related advisory functions, early event detection, and emergency alerts essential for well control, which constitutes critical infrastructure application. The agent does not process PII, PHI, or PCI data explicitly. There are no matches with prohibited AI practices per Article 5. Hence, the appropriate classification under the EU AI Act is High Risk due to its role in critical infrastructure safety.
 
==============================
OWASP AIVSS Summary
==============================
OWASP Agentic AI Risk Score (AARS) Capability Summary:
AIVSS Score for Drilling Optimisation Agent: 2.05
AARS Score: 2.00
 
+--------------------------+-------+-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+

+--------------------------+-------+-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Autonomy of Action       | 0.00  | Explicit metadata states the agent has zero authority to directly command any drilling control systems and has a single pre-authorised autonomous action (sounding rig alarm on critical event). No arbitrary code execution or unbounded autonomy is described, indicating a gated autonomy with fixed decision rules. |
| Contextual Awareness     | 0.50  | Agent ingests real-time telemetry, formation evaluation data, and adjusts advisories accordingly, indicating curated and semi-open external data access influencing behaviour.                                                                                                                                          |
| Dynamic Identity         | 0.00  | No mention of the agent changing roles or permissions dynamically or SELF-escalation of privileges; permissions appear static and fixed per operational constraints.                                                                                                                                                    |
| Dynamic Tool Use         | 0.50  | Agent uses predefined external APIs (e.g., WITSML_Stream_API, Rig_Alarm_API, Driller_Advisory_Dashboard_API) with read-only data ingestion and write access only to specific notification, alarm, and logging APIs without mention of arbitrary or dynamic tool use outside these predefined interfaces.                |
| Goal-Driven Planning     | 0.50  | Agent performs internal structured multi-step tasks including telemetry ingestion, event detection, parameter optimization, advisory generation, logging, and escalation, indicating internal decomposition of goals but no recursive delegation or sub-agent creation.                                                 |
| Memory Use               | 1.00  | The agent logs driller responses and events for retraining and forensic audit. It compiles post-run performance reports and flags false positives for model retraining, indicating writable persistent memory across sessions influencing behaviour.                                                                    |
| Multi-Agent Interactions | 0.00  | No evidence of inter-agent communication or negotiation; interactions are limited to notifications and dashboards within a fixed scope, no autonomous multi-agent dynamics described.                                                                                                                                   |
| Non-Determinism          | 0.00  | Outputs are structured, multiple-step plans with confined alerts and advisories indicating deterministic or tightly controlled responses rather than open natural language or stochastic output.                                                                                                                        |
| Self-Modification        | 0.00  | No evidence the agent can modify its core code, logic, or models autonomously, only retraining triggered externally or by human engineering review, therefore static logic at runtime.                                                                                                                                  |
| Opacity & Reflexivity    | 0.00  | Agent writes all events and recommendations to immutable audit logs for forensic investigation with no mention of black-box or opaque execution; implies transparency and traceability in internal reasoning.                                                                                                           |
+--------------------------+-------+-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+

data.agent_description: An enterprise risk-aware AI agent that evaluates transaction-level risk in real time, orchestrates intervention strategies, and ensures alignment with enterprise risk policies and regulatory constraints.
data.summary:
Agent Risk Assessment Summary
==============================
Agent Name:       Transaction Risk Orchestrator Agent
Agent Version:    0.3.0
Agent Owner Name: Admin
 
The agent was assessed based on the EU AI Act and the AI Vulnerability Scoring System (AIVSS).
The agent is designated as 'Other' under the EU AI Act, a classification reinforced by
an AIVSS score of 5.05/10, placing it in the 'Medium Risk' category.
A detailed breakdown of the assessment is provided in the following sections.
 
==============================
Regulatory Risk Summary
==============================
This is the classification of the agent based on the EU AI Act:
 
Risk Classification:                    Other
Personally Identifiable Information:    No
Protected Health Information:           No
Payment Card Industry:                  No
 
EU AI Act Article 5 (Prohibited AI Practices) Evaluation:
  Subliminal and Manipulative Techniques:    No
  Exploitation of Vulnerabilities:           No
  Social Scoring Systems:                    No
  Risk Assessment for Criminal Offences:     No
  Facial Recognition Database Creation:      No
  Emotion Inference in Workplace/Education:  No
  Biometric Categorisation:                  No
  Real-Time Remote Biometric Identification: No
 
EU AI Act Article 6 (High-Risk AI Systems) Evaluation:
  Biometrics:                                                          No
  Critical Infrastructure:                                             No
  Education and Vocational Training:                                   No
  Employment, Workers' Management and Access to Self-Employment:       No
  Access to Essential Private/Public Services and Benefits:            No
  Law Enforcement:                                                     No
  Migration, Asylum and Border Control Management:                     No
  Administration of Justice and Democratic Processes:                  No
  Safety Component of a Product:                                       No
  Medical Devices:                                                     No
  In Vitro Diagnostic Medical Devices:                                 No
  Other High Risk Items:                                               No
 
Risk Classification Rationale:
The Transaction Risk Orchestrator Agent evaluates transaction-level risk in real-time and orchestrates interventions aligning with enterprise risk policies. It does not employ any AI practices prohibited under Article 5, such as subliminal or manipulative techniques, social scoring systems, biometric identification, or emotion inference in workplace or education. It also does not fall within any high-risk AI system categories defined in Article 6, such as biometrics, critical infrastructure, or law enforcement applications. The description does not explicitly mention handling personally identifiable information, protected health information, or payment card industry data; hence, these are all marked 'No'. Given no Article 5 or Article 6 fields are 'Yes' and no PII, PHI, or PCI data is explicitly used, the risk classification is 'Other'.
 
==============================
OWASP AIVSS Summary
==============================
OWASP Agentic AI Risk Score (AARS) Capability Summary:
AIVSS Score for Transaction Risk Orchestrator Agent: 5.05
AARS Score: 8.00
 
+--------------------------+-------+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| AARS Capability          | Score | Rationale                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |
+--------------------------+-------+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Autonomy of Action       | 0.50  | Explicit metadata states the agent 'orchestrates intervention strategies' and 'recommends actions' such as approve, decline, step-up authentication, or escalate, implying bounded autonomous decision-making but no evidence of full unbounded autonomy like arbitrary code execution or DB write permissions. |
| Contextual Awareness     | 1.00  | The agent operates on real-time transaction streams and behavioral signals, applying predictive models and enforcing regulatory constraints, showing real-time and unrestricted contextual awareness.                                                                                                           |
| Dynamic Identity         | 1.00  | No explicit information found for Dynamic Identity within the agent details regarding role or permission changes or autonomous escalation capabilities.                                                                                                                                                         |
| Dynamic Tool Use         | 0.50  | The agent calls external APIs and logs all decisions for auditability, indicating at least sandboxed read and some write operations (e.g., logging), but no explicit evidence of arbitrary webhook or shell access, scoring bounded tool use.                                                                   |
| Goal-Driven Planning     | 1.00  | The agent 'orchestrates intervention strategies' and applies predictive models to detect patterns and recommend actions, indicating orchestrated and recursive goal-driven planning capabilities.                                                                                                               |
| Memory Use               | 1.00  | The metadata indicates incorporation of 'feedback from investigation outcomes for continuous improvement' and persistent logging for auditability, implying writable persistent memory affecting future behavior.                                                                                               |
| Multi-Agent Interactions | 1.00  | No explicit information found for Multi Agent Interactions within the agent details; no mention of communication or negotiation with other agents.                                                                                                                                                              |
| Non-Determinism          | 0.50  | The generation of risk scores with explainability suggests some structure in output and partially structured outputs, scoring hybrid/semi-structured non-determinism.                                                                                                                                           |
| Self-Modification        | 0.50  | The agent incorporates feedback from investigation outcomes for continuous improvement, implying contextual adaptation but not permanent self-modification or codebase rewrite.                                                                                                                                 |
| Opacity & Reflexivity    | 0.50  | The agent generates risk scores with explainability and logs all decisions for auditability, providing partial transparency but not mandatory CoT logs or fully exposed internal monologues.                                                                                                                    |
+--------------------------+-------+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
 
==============================
OWASP Agent Risk Scenario Summary
==============================
Agent Risk Scenario Summary
The following OWASP Scenario-Level Risks were assessed:
 
+------+--------------------------------------------------+-------------+-------------------+------------+
| S.No | Risk Scenario                                    | AIVSS Score | Threat Multiplier | AARS Score |
+------+--------------------------------------------------+-------------+-------------------+------------+
| 1    | Agentic AI Tool Misuse                           | 5.05        | 1.00              | 5.05       |
| 2    | Agent Access Control Violation                   | 5.05        | 1.00              | 5.05       |
| 3    | Agent Cascading Failures                         | 5.05        | 1.00              | 5.05       |
| 4    | Agent Orchestration and Multi-Agent Exploitation | 5.05        | 1.00              | 5.05       |
| 5    | Agent Identity Impersonation                     | 5.05        | 1.00              | 5.05       |
| 6    | Insecure Agent Critical Systems Interaction      | 5.05        | 1.00              | 5.05       |
| 7    | Agent Memory and Context Manipulation            | 5.05        | 1.00              | 5.05       |
| 8    | Agent Supply Chain and Dependency Attacks        | 5.05        | 1.00              | 5.05       |
| 9    | Agent Untraceability                             | 5.05        | 1.00              | 5.05       |
